name: m-vscode
description: M Language Tools (MUMPS) — VS Code extension for pure, portable M: language registration, tree-sitter syntax, an LSP client over `m lsp`, and Test Explorer / coverage / engine execution over the `m` CLI. No VistA required.

scripts.check: npm run check-wasm && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test:cov && npm run bundle
scripts.test:vscode: npm run bundle && npm run bundle:smoke && node --import tsx src/smoke/run.ts

author: Rafael Richards

commands.title: M: Execute Selection on the Engine

mLanguageTools.enable.description: Run the M language server (`m lsp`) for diagnostics and formatting.

mLanguageTools.serverPath.description: Path to the `m` executable. Resolved on PATH when left as `m`.

mLanguageTools.serverArgs.description: Arguments passed to the server executable.

mLanguageTools.lint.profile.markdownDescription: Lint profile override. Leave empty (recommended) to use the project's `.m-cli.toml` — the same file `m lint` and CI read, which is what makes editor diagnostics identical to CI diagnostics. Run **M: Configure M Profile** to write that file if the *M lint profile* status item says none governs your project. **Note:** `m lsp` does not yet honour an override sent by the client; until it does (tracked as a P3 change in m-cli) this setting has no effect and the project config governs.

mLanguageTools.diagnostics.debounceMs.description: Milliseconds to wait after you stop typing before re-linting. The server re-lints the whole document per change, so this bounds how often that happens.

mLanguageTools.diagnostics.largeFileByte.markdownDescription: Documents this size or larger are linted **on save only**, not as you type — a 1 MB routine costs seconds per lint. Set to `0` to always lint live. The chosen mode is announced in the *M Language Tools* output channel.

mLanguageTools.engine.markdownDescription: Which M engine the Test Explorer, coverage, and *Execute Selection* reach, via `m --engine`. The extension never talks to an engine directly — every engine operation shells out to the `m` CLI, which owns the driver seam.

mLanguageTools.docker.markdownDescription: Name of a running Docker container holding the engine (e.g. `vehu` for YottaDB, `foia-t12` for IRIS). Passed as `m --docker`. Leave empty to let the driver resolve the connection from its own `M_<ENGINE>_*` environment.

mLanguageTools.namespace.markdownDescription: IRIS namespace for test and coverage runs (`m --namespace`). Ignored for YottaDB. Leave empty for the CLI default.

mLanguageTools.engine.lockWaitSeconds.markdownDescription: How long *Execute Selection* waits for the engine run-lock before giving up and naming the holder. `0` means do not wait. The wait is always bounded — a run-lock must never look like a hang.
